hero.headline: Empowering the next generation of developers
hero.subheadline: We believe coding should be accessible, fun, and community-driven.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: IT Learn was created with a simple goal: to strip away the complexity of learning to code. We provide a structured, interactive path for beginners to go from 'Hello World' to building real applications.

cards.title: Community First
cards.description: Learning is harder alone. We build tools that connect learners.

cards.title: Practice over Theory
cards.description: You can't learn to swim by reading a book. We focus on interactive exercises.

cards.title: Open for Everyone
cards.description: We can't make high-quality education completely free, but we offer a free plan.

story.title: Our Story
story.text:
IT Learn is a Dutch learning platform founded in 2025 by Broodje56 and Joren, with the mission to make learning programming accessible and enjoyable.

Many learning platforms are overly complex or too theoretical. IT Learn focuses on learning by doing, using clear explanations, interactive lessons, and a motivating reward system.

At IT Learn, it’s not just about knowledge, but about understanding, growth, and community.

communityPage.headline: Learn together
communityPage.text: Coding is easier when you’re not alone. Join our Discord to connect with other learners.

sections.title: 1. Introduction and Acceptance
paragraphs: Welcome to IT Learn. These Terms and Conditions ("Terms") constitute a legally binding agreement between you and IT Learn governing your access to and use of the IT Learn platform, website, and services (collectively, the "Service").
By creating an account, accessing, or using our Service, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms, as well as our Privacy Policy.
If you do not agree to these Terms, you must not access or use our Service.
If you are under 18 years of age, you confirm that you have obtained parental or guardian consent to use the Service.

sections.title: 2. User Accounts and Eligibility
paragraphs: Eligibility: You must be at least 13 years old to create an account. Users between 13 and 18 years old must have parental or guardian permission.

Account Creation: To access certain features, you must create an account by providing accurate, current, and complete information. You may register using your email address or through third-party authentication services (such as Google OAuth).
Account Security: You are solely responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account. You agree to:
bullets: Keep your password secure and confidential
Notify us immediately of any unauthorized access or security breach
Not share your account with others
Not create multiple accounts or impersonate others

sections.title: Account Termination
paragraphs: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account if you violate these Terms or engage in conduct that we determine to be harmful to other users or our Service.

sections.title: 3. Copyright
paragraphs: You are not allowed to copy or reuse any part of our code, for either commercial or non-commercial purposes. All code on this website is protected by our copyright.

sections.title: 4. Acceptable Use and Prohibited Activities
paragraphs: You agree to use the Service only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You specifically agree NOT to:
bullets: Use the Service for any illegal activities or to promote illegal conduct
Distribute malware, viruses, or other harmful code
Violate the intellectual property rights, privacy rights, or other rights of others
Harass, threaten, defame, or abuse other users
Attempt to gain unauthorized access to our systems, servers, or networks
Interfere with or disrupt the Service or servers/networks connected to the Service
Use automated systems (bots, scrapers) to access the Service without permission
Submit false, misleading, or fraudulent information
Circumvent any access restrictions or security measures
Resell, redistribute, or commercially exploit the Service without authorization
Use the Service to spam, phish, or engage in other forms of unsolicited communication

sections.title: Investigation and Enforcement
paragraphs: We reserve the right to investigate violations, suspend or terminate accounts, and report illegal activity to law enforcement authorities.

sections.title: 5. Intellectual Property
paragraphs: All content and software on the site are owned by IT Learn or its licensors and are protected by copyright and other laws. Nothing in these Terms grants you ownership rights to such content.

sections.title: 6. Disclaimers and Limitation of Liability
paragraphs: Service "As Is": The Service is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement. We do not guarantee that the Service will be uninterrupted, secure, or error-free.

Educational Content: While we strive to provide accurate and high-quality educational content, we make no warranties about the completeness, reliability, or accuracy of the content. You are responsible for verifying information and should not rely solely on our content for professional or critical decisions.
Limitation of Liability: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law:
bullets: Our total liability for any claims arising from your use of the Service is limited to the amount you have paid us in the past 12 months, or €100, whichever is greater
We are not liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, data, use, or other intangible losses

We are not liable for damages resulting from: unauthorized access to your account, interruptions or errors in the Service, third-party content or conduct, or your reliance on content obtained through the Service

sections.title: Mandatory Rights
paragraphs: Nothing in these Terms excludes or limits our liability for fraud, gross negligence, death, or personal injury caused by our negligence, or any other liability that cannot be excluded under applicable law. Consumers in the EU retain all mandatory statutory rights under consumer protection law.

sections.title: 7. Changes
paragraphs: We may modify these Terms from time to time. We will announce significant changes in advance. By continuing to use the Site after the change, you accept the updates.

sections.title: 8. Applicable Law
paragraphs: These Terms and Conditions are governed by the law of the country in which IT Learn is established. This country is Belgium in Europe, unless otherwise required by mandatory law.

sections.title: 9. Service Availability and Modifications
paragraphs: We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue the Service (or any part thereof) at any time, with or without notice. We will not be liable to you or any third party for any modification, suspension, or discontinuation of the Service.
We may perform maintenance, updates, or improvements that may temporarily affect Service availability. We will make reasonable efforts to provide advance notice of planned maintenance when possible.

sections.title: 10. Indemnification
paragraphs: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless IT Learn, its affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees, and agents from any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, costs, or expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising from:
bullets: Your use of the Service
Your violation of these Terms
Your violation of any rights of another party
Any content you submit or transmit through the Service

sections.title: 11. Dispute Resolution and Governing Law
paragraphs: Governing Law: These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Belgium, without regard to its conflict of law provisions. The United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods does not apply.
Jurisdiction: Subject to mandatory consumer protection laws, any disputes arising from these Terms or your use of the Service shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Belgium.

Consumer Rights: If you are a consumer in the European Union, you retain the right to bring proceedings in your country of residence, and nothing in these Terms affects your mandatory statutory rights under EU consumer protection law.

sections.title: 12. Severability
paragraphs: If any provision of these Terms is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect. The invalid provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid and enforceable.

sections.title: 13. Entire Agreement
paragraphs: These Terms, together with our Privacy Policy, constitute the entire agreement between you and IT Learn regarding the Service and supersede all prior agreements, understandings, and communications, whether written or oral.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Statement
privacyPage.controller: Controller: IT Learn | contact email: contact.itlearn@gmail.com

sections.title: 1. What personal data do we process?
paragraphs: We only process the following personal data:
bullets: Account information: email address, chosen username, and password (hashed).

Authentication information: data provided by third-party authentication services including Google OAuth (email address and account ID), GitHub (username, email, profile information), and Discord (username, email, Discord ID).
Technical information: IP address and server logs (temporary, for security and debugging purposes).
Chat data: When you use the Tawk.to chat feature, we process your messages, name (if provided), and metadata for customer support purposes.

sections.title: 2. Purposes and Legal Basis
paragraphs: We process personal data for the following purposes, in accordance with Article 6 of the GDPR:
bullets: Account creation and login (authentication) - Legal basis: Performance of contract (Article 6(1)(b) GDPR). This is necessary to provide you with access to our educational platform.

Security and misuse prevention - Legal basis: Legitimate interest (Article 6(1)(f) GDPR), specifically fraud detection, abuse prevention, and technical troubleshooting to protect our platform and users.
Communication and processing of requests - Legal basis: Performance of contract and legitimate interest to respond to user inquiries and provide customer support.
Legal compliance - Legal basis: Legal obligation (Article 6(1)(c) GDPR) to comply with applicable laws and regulations.

sections.title: No marketing use
paragraphs: We do not use personal data for newsletters without explicit consent, targeted advertising, profiling for marketing purposes, or selling data to third parties.

sections.title: 3. Processors / External Service Providers
paragraphs: We use the following third-party service providers to operate our platform:
bullets: Supabase - Database and authentication services; our project is located in Ireland (EU). Supabase complies with GDPR and provides data processing agreements.
Google (OAuth) - Third-party authentication service for user login. When you sign in with Google, we receive your email address and Google account ID. Subject to Google's Privacy Policy.

GitHub (OAuth) - Third-party authentication service for user login. When you sign in with GitHub, we receive your GitHub username, email address, and public profile information. Subject to GitHub's Privacy Policy.

Discord (OAuth) - Third-party authentication service for user login. When you sign in with Discord, we receive your Discord username, email address, and Discord user ID. Subject to Discord's Privacy Policy.

Cloudflare - Content delivery network (CDN) and hosting services. Cloudflare operates globally but complies with GDPR and EU data protection standards. Cloudflare may process IP addresses and technical data.
PythonAnywhere - Backend server infrastructure for running application logic. Servers are typically located in the US; Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) apply for data transfers outside the EU.

Tawk.to - Live chat support widget. When you use the chat feature, Tawk.to processes your messages, name (if provided), and metadata. Tawk.to complies with GDPR; you can choose not to use the chat feature.

sections.title: Your choices
paragraphs: You can choose which authentication method to use (email/password, Google, GitHub, or Discord). The use of the Tawk.to chat feature is optional.

sections.title: 4. Retention Periods
bullets: Account data: as long as the account is active + 1 year after deletion.
Server logs/IP addresses: 30 days (security and debugging).
Uploads (if added later): as long as the account is active or up to 1 year after deletion.

sections.title: 5. Minors and Age Policy
paragraphs: IT Learn allows registration for those aged 13 and over. In Belgium, the digital age limit is 13. We never ask for ID photos and do not perform ID verification.

sections.title: 6. User Rights (GDPR Rights)
paragraphs: Under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have the following rights:
bullets: Right of access (Article 15) - You can request a copy of your personal data we hold.
Right to rectification (Article 16) - You can request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data.
Right to erasure/"right to be forgotten" (Article 17) - You can request deletion of your personal data under certain conditions.
Right to restriction of processing (Article 18) - You can request that we limit how we use your data.
Right to data portability (Article 20) - You can request your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
Right to object (Article 21) - You can object to processing based on legitimate interests.
Right to withdraw consent (Article 7(3)) - Where processing is based on consent, you can withdraw it at any time.
Right not to be subject to automated decision-making (Article 22) - Protection against solely automated decisions with legal effects.

sections.title: Exercising your rights
paragraphs: To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at: contact.itlearn@gmail.com. We will respond to your request within one month, as required by GDPR.
If you are not satisfied with how we handle your request or have concerns about our data practices, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Belgian Data Protection Authority (APD/GBA):
Address: Rue de la Presse 35, 1000 Brussels, Belgium
Email: contact@apd-gba.be
Website: www.dataprotectionauthority.be

sections.title: 7. Account Deletion
paragraphs: Once the feature is available, users can delete their account themselves through their settings. Until then, a request can be sent to contact.itlearn@gmail.com; we will delete the data according to the retention periods.

sections.title: 8. Security Measures
paragraphs: We take appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorized access, loss, destruction, or alteration, including:
bullets: HTTPS/TLS encryption - All data transmitted between your browser and our servers is encrypted.
Secure authentication - Supabase, Google OAuth, GitHub OAuth, and Discord OAuth for secure login.
Password protection - Passwords are hashed using industry-standard algorithms and never stored in plain text.
Limited data retention - Server logs and IP addresses are retained for a maximum of 30 days.
Access controls - Restricted access to personal data on a need-to-know basis.
Regular security reviews - Ongoing monitoring and updates to security practices.
Third-party security - All service providers are vetted for GDPR compliance and security standards.
Uploads are currently disabled.

sections.title: Security notice
paragraphs: While we implement strong security measures, no method of transmission over the internet or electronic storage is 100% secure. We cannot guarantee absolute security but commit to promptly addressing any security incidents.

sections.title: 9. Cookies and Trackers
paragraphs: Only necessary cookies for login and site functionality. No ads or analytics. Supabase or the hosting provider may place functional cookies.

sections.title: 10. Transfer outside the EU
paragraphs: Supabase operates in Ireland (EU). If data is processed outside the EU in the future, we will use appropriate safeguards (SCCs).

sections.title: 11. Data Breaches
paragraphs: If we suspect a data breach, we will investigate it and report it if legally required.

sections.title: 12. Changes
paragraphs: We may amend this statement; if material changes are made, we will publish a new version with an effective date.

sections.title: 13. Contact Information
paragraphs: Data Controller: IT Learn
General inquiries and data protection requests: contact.itlearn@gmail.com
Abuse and security reports: abuse-ithelp-be@googlegroups.com

For data protection inquiries, privacy rights requests, or legal matters, please use contact.itlearn@gmail.com. We aim to respond to all inquiries within 30 days. For official or legal processing, authorized representatives will handle your request and provide appropriate contact details upon request.
